name: Rainforests
description: The world's rainforests — their layers, iconic species, and conservation challenges

questions.q: How many distinct vertical layers is a rainforest typically divided into?

source.notes: Emergent, canopy, understory, and forest floor.

questions.q: Which is the world's largest tropical rainforest?

source.notes: About 5.5 million km² across nine countries.

questions.q: The majority of the Amazon rainforest lies within which country?

source.notes: Roughly 60% of the Amazon basin is in Brazil.

questions.q: Which great ape, native to the rainforests of Borneo and Sumatra, has distinctive orange-red fur?

source.notes: Name means 'person of the forest' in Malay/Indonesian.

questions.q: On which continent is the Amazon rainforest located?

source.notes: Spans Brazil, Peru, Colombia, Venezuela, Ecuador, Bolivia, Guyana, Suriname, and French Guiana.

questions.q: Tropical rainforests are typically found close to which imaginary line?

source.notes: Roughly between the Tropics of Cancer and Capricorn.

questions.q: What kind of animal is the harpy eagle, native to Central and South American rainforests?

source.notes: One of the largest and most powerful raptors in the world.

questions.q: Which topmost layer of the rainforest consists of scattered very tall trees rising above the rest?

source.notes: Trees here can reach 50-60 meters tall, exposed above the canopy.

questions.q: Which primate group, ranging from tiny mouse species to the larger indri, is found only in Madagascar's rainforests?

source.notes: About 100+ species endemic to Madagascar.

questions.q: Which Australian state is home to the ancient Daintree Rainforest?

source.notes: Estimated to be about 180 million years old — older than the Amazon.

questions.q: What is the largest species of snake native to the Amazon rainforest?

source.notes: Can exceed 5 meters and 100 kg; the heaviest snake species.

questions.q: Approximately what percentage of Earth's land surface is covered by rainforests?

source.notes: Yet they're estimated to host over half of the world's known species.

questions.q: The Congo rainforest, the world's second-largest tropical rainforest, is on which continent?

source.notes: Spans six Central African countries.

questions.q: Which primate, the largest in the world, lives in the rainforests of Central Africa?

source.notes: Both lowland and mountain gorilla species are critically endangered.

questions.q: Which flightless bird with vibrant blue skin and a tall head casque lives in New Guinea and northeastern Australian rainforests?

source.notes: Considered one of the world's most dangerous birds due to its powerful kicks.

questions.q: Which semi-aquatic South American animal is the world's largest rodent?

source.notes: Can weigh up to ~65 kg; closely related to guinea pigs.

questions.q: Which Amazon apex predator is the largest cat species in the Americas?

source.notes: Has the strongest bite of any big cat relative to size.

questions.q: The Amazon River carries approximately what fraction of the world's fresh river water?

source.notes: The largest river system in the world by volume.

questions.q: What is the world's smallest monkey species, native to the Amazon basin?

source.notes: Adults weigh about 100 grams — roughly the weight of a chocolate bar.

questions.q: The cacao tree (source of chocolate) originates from the rainforests of which region?

source.notes: Theobroma cacao domesticated in the Amazon over 5,000 years ago.

questions.q: Wild poison dart frogs get their potent toxicity primarily from what part of their diet?
choices: Specific ants and mites they eat

source.notes: Captive-bred frogs fed different diets lose their toxicity entirely.

questions.q: Which iridescent blue-winged Amazonian butterfly, one of the world's largest, is named for a Greek epithet of Aphrodite?

source.notes: Wingspan up to 20 cm; the blue is a structural effect, not pigment.
